misconceptionSummary: Students divide the perimeter by 2 instead of by n when finding the side length.

lines.text: Divided by 2 — confused with a rectangle

steps.narration: The student divided by two as if the shape were a rectangle. A rectangle divides by two because it has two pairs of equal sides. An octagon has eight equal sides — the perimeter divides by eight.

visuals.ruleText: Side length = Perimeter ÷ number of sides (n)
visuals.subText: Count the sides of the polygon before dividing

steps.narration: Always count the sides of the polygon first. The perimeter is shared equally between all n sides — divide by n, not by two.
Eight sides: divide fifty-six by eight to get seven centimetres per side. Check by multiplying back: eight times seven is fifty-six. Correct.

visuals.label: Divide perimeter by the number of sides — not by 2

steps.narration: Seven centimetres. Count the sides first, then divide. Always check by multiplying back.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: dividing the perimeter by 2 for a regular octagon. Divide by the number of sides — 8 here. The ÷2 shortcut only applies to rectangles.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Dividing by 2 is a rectangle trick. For any regular polygon, divide by the number of sides. Count the sides first — then divide.
